seoTitle: Hazel Park vs Hazelwood — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hazel Park and Hazelwood. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hazel Park vs Hazelwood: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hazel Park and Hazelwood.
heading: Hazel Park vs Hazelwood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hazel Park, Victoria and Hazelwood, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hazel Park has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Hazelwood (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hazel Park or Hazelwood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hazel Park has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Hazelwood scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hazel Park scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Hazelwood scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hazel Park or Hazelwood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hazel Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
